all.title: Self-Supervised Role Identity Acquisition from Interaction History
all.rationale: All branches in this tree assume explicit character profiles must be provided. The field needs a method that can infer role identity autonomously from interaction data without curated profiles.
all.meta_gap: No existing method in this lineage can establish role-play consistency without requiring explicit character profiles as input.
all.shared_mechanism: All branches inherit the assumption that character profiles are static, externally-provided inputs.
all.non_obvious_property: Property: role identity must be represented as a latent variable that can be learned from conversational context alone, without any external profile annotations. Obvious composition that fails: Persona-Plug (explicit persona injection) + a persona extraction module (e.g., PersonaChat extraction). This fails because extraction modules still require explicit persona descriptions in the training data, and cannot generalize to unseen characters with no predefined profile. Why prior work has not formalized this property: The field has treated profile injection as a necessary input rather than a learned representation, so no method has attempted to infer role identity purely from interaction dynamics without any profile annotations at all.

all.title: Selective Role Activation Minimizing Profile Dependency
all.rationale: The trajectory across branches is moving from static profile embeddings to dynamic per-step role activation (as done by the frontier). After this trajectory completes (dynamic activation is standard), the next bottleneck is that activation still requires explicit profile features. The future direction is to design role activation mechanisms that rely minimally on external profile information.
all.trend: From static persona embedding to dynamic role-aware reasoning via profile injection
all.trend_endpoint: Dynamic role activation becomes the standard approach, but still requiring explicit profiles.
all.implied_next_problem: The need for profile-free role activation, where the model learns to self-activate role identity without any external profile input.
all.non_obvious_property: Property: role activation must be driven entirely by the interaction context and a learned latent identity variable, not by explicit profile features. Obvious composition that fails: Frontier's RIA + a profile-free constraint loss (e.g., mutual information minimization). This fails because RIA's architecture explicitly concatenates profile features with reasoning traces, so removing those features would collapse the activation mechanism. Why prior work has not formalized this property: Prior work treats profile features as necessary activation keys; no method has designed activation that works without any key at inference time.
